info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the \"Info and Links\".

q1.prompt: A patient with Human African Trypanosomiasis (HAT) progresses from the initial hemolymphatic stage to the neurological stage. Which of the following clinical presentations is most characteristic of this stage of the infection?

options.label: Acute hemolytic anemia and hemoglobinuria
Development of painful, indurated skin ulcers (chancre)
Massive splenomegaly and spiking fever cycles
Progressive somnolence, slurred speech, and coma
Unilateral painless periorbital swelling (Romaña sign)

q2.prompt: A healthy teenager develops rapidly progressive and fatal encephalitis. Brain tissue examination reveals cysts and trophozoites of a free-living ameba. Which of the following characteristics distinguishes Balamuthia mandrillaris from other free-living amebae causing central nervous system disease?

options.label: It enters the host primarily via the cribriform plate during diving
It exclusively infects immunocompromised individuals with HIV
It is acquired through contact with soil, air, or water reservoirs
It is only found in stagnant tropical freshwater ponds
It lacks a cyst stage in its life cycle within human tissue

q3.prompt: A laboratory technician is examining a thick blood film to differentiate between the microfilariae of Wuchereria bancrofti and Brugia malayi. Which of the following morphological features is the definitive hallmark of Brugia malayi?

options.label: Absence of a protective sheath around the larva
Presence of exactly two terminal nuclei at the end of the tail
Short cephalic space with a 1:1 ratio to the body width
Smoothly curved body appearance without twisting
Uniformly arranged nuclei that do not overlap

q4.prompt: A 68-year-old patient who regularly consumes raw freshwater fish presents with symptoms of megaloblastic anemia. Which of the following mechanisms best explains the development of Vitamin B12 deficiency in this parasitic infection?

options.label: Chronic blood loss due to attachment to the large intestine
Destruction of gastric parietal cells and loss of intrinsic factor
Direct competition for and depletion of host B12 in the intestine
Malabsorption caused by the internal autoinfection of larvae
Systemic inflammatory response to migrating rhabditiform larvae

q5.prompt: Adult schistosomes are able to reside within the human intravascular system for long periods without being eradicated by the host's immune system. Which of the following molecular mechanisms best explains this immune evasion?

options.label: Inhibition of NF-κB to prevent host cell apoptosis
Masking by incorporating host MHC and blood group antigens
Rapid VSG-mediated antigenic variation of the surface coat
Secretion of hyaluronidase to penetrate the vascular endothelium
Sequestration within sequestered splenic macrophages

q6.prompt: A traveler returning from a tropical beach vacation presents with a painful, inflamed lesion on the sole of the foot. The physician identifies the dark posterior portion of a flea embedded in the skin. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

options.label: Cutaneous larva migrans
Leishmaniasis
Pediculosis
Scabies
Tungiasis
